reason: Global TianChen-RV architecture and RVV-first authority chain for the macro campaign.
RVV plugin-local primitive/resource/selected-body/route contracts, including low-precision contraction and fail-closed rules.
Common EmitC lowerable-route boundary and prohibition on common semantic inference.
Archived grouped u2 campaign evidence and next-gap attribution context.

summary: Gate 3 positive packed-i4 structural resource slice: added signed packed-i4-in-i8 resource candidates, explicit selected-candidate consumption in Gearbox/realization, route-family/provider expected packed facts, and fail-closed statement planning at the missing nibble unpack boundary.

Gate 3 packed-i4 nibble-unpack statement slice: direct-contraction statement planning now accepts the selected signed packed-i4 resource facts, emits RVV-owned low/high nibble sign-extension steps for both operands, runs the low/high widening product-reduction sequence, builds a lowerable route, and keeps target artifact export fail-closed at the packed-i4 Gate 4 boundary.

Gate 4 packed-i4 artifact/export slice: replaced the target-layer packed-i4 hard fail-close with artifact route-family validation that consumes provider-owned packed-i4 resource facts and validates the rebuilt low/high nibble sign-extension statement payload before target export acceptance. Added a realized pre-route target test fixture and positive/stale packed-i4 artifact validation coverage without claiming generated-bundle or runtime evidence.

Gate 4 packed-i4 generated-bundle evidence slice: fixed the public selected-body consumer boundary to accept the packed-i4 resource decision, updated generated-bundle evidence tooling to validate v4 low-precision resource metadata dynamically for default byte/grouped and explicit signed packed-i4 selections, added focused packed-i4 pre-realized input coverage, and kept the evidence dry-run/artifact-only with no runtime or ssh rvv claim.

Gate 5 packed-i4 executable correctness slice: updated generated-bundle harness generation to switch to a packed-i4 scalar reference oracle only after provider-owned object/header low-precision resource metadata validates the accepted signed packed-i4 selected candidate. The harness now packs two signed i4 lanes per int8 byte, sign-extends low/high nibbles, accumulates both products per packed byte, applies runtime f32 scale, and records same-target scalar/reference comparison while keeping timing unclaimed. Updated the RVV plugin spec with the executable packed-i4 harness/oracle contract.
